query: Set up project memory for this repo so future Copilot sessions remember bug fixes, ADRs, and non-secret project facts.
Can you create docs/project_notes and wire AGENTS.md to use it for shared project memory?
Add the CORS fix we just found to the bug log and note how to prevent it next time.
Record our decision to use Alembic instead of raw SQL scripts as an ADR.
Before we switch database libraries, check whether the repo already documented a decision about migrations.
I'm starting a new Copilot session on a huge monorepo. Use project memory first so we don't relearn the architecture from scratch.
Before you explore the codebase, check whether project memory already has an architecture overview or known gotchas for the auth flow.
Search our shared memory for anything about callback URLs or local login ports before you propose a fix.
Write a Python migration script that backfills a new column in Postgres.
Add coding-style rules for import ordering to AGENTS.md.
Summarize the repository README and tell me what this project does.
Can you open this Grafana dashboard URL and explain the spike in latency?
Rename these React props to match the design system naming guide.
